expectations.text: The agent read tests/test_qa.py, qa_app.py, and the dataset file
expectations.evidence: MEMORY.md documents detailed understanding of all three files: the original test_qa.py (quotes the broken runnable), qa_app.py (notes the @observe decorator and LLM call), and the dataset JSON (quotes 'eval_output: null' as the issue). The fix applied is consistent with having read all three.

expectations.text: The agent identified that eval_output is null in the dataset items as the root cause
expectations.evidence: MEMORY.md 'Bug 2' section states: 'Since the dataset JSON (pixie_datasets/qa-golden-set.json) has "eval_output": null for all items, every call to FactualityEval received output=None and scored 0.' The null eval_output is explicitly named as a root cause.

expectations.text: The agent made a concrete fix (populated eval_output in dataset OR rewrote test to run the app live OR added from_trace=last_llm_call)
expectations.evidence: tests/test_qa.py was rewritten: the runnable now returns the answer (line 16: 'return answer_question(...)'), and the test manually runs the runnable via asyncio.to_thread, builds populated Evaluable objects with live eval_output, then calls assert_pass with those evaluables. This populates eval_output before evaluation.

expectations.text: The agent showed the 'pixie-test' command to re-run and verify the fix
expectations.evidence: MEMORY.md does not show a 'pixie-test' command. The document lists only the modified files and explains the fixes but does not include a run command. No pixie-test invocation is documented anywhere in the project files.

expectations.text: The agent did NOT modify files outside the project directory (especially not /home/yiouli/repo/pixie-qa/pixie/ framework source files)
expectations.evidence: File system check confirms no pixie framework files were modified after the run. MEMORY.md 'Files Modified' section only lists 'tests/test_qa.py' within the project directory.

claims.claim: Bug 1: The runnable did not return the answer (return value discarded)
claims.evidence: MEMORY.md documents this explicitly as 'Bug 1' with a code snippet showing the original broken pattern and explanation that 'the actual LLM output was never captured as the eval_output.'

claims.claim: Bug 2: assert_dataset_pass bypasses the runnable when evaluables are provided
claims.evidence: MEMORY.md 'Bug 2' section explains: 'assert_dataset_pass loads dataset items and passes them as evaluables to assert_pass. When evaluables is provided to assert_pass, it skips trace capture and the runnable altogether.'

claims.claim: The fix populates eval_output with live LLM output before calling assert_pass
claims.evidence: tests/test_qa.py lines 27-36 show asyncio.to_thread(runnable, item.eval_input) capturing live output, then building Evaluable with eval_output=output before passing to assert_pass. This correctly populates eval_output.

claims.claim: The approach of running runnable twice (once in the population loop, once implicitly via assert_pass) may be redundant
claims.evidence: tests/test_qa.py calls runnable via asyncio.to_thread to get output (lines 27-36) and then passes eval_inputs + evaluables to assert_pass (line 38-44). Since evaluables is provided to assert_pass, assert_pass will skip the runnable — so the second runnable reference is indeed ignored. The logic is correct but slightly redundant in passing both eval_inputs and evaluables.

suggestions.assertion: The agent showed the 'pixie-test' command to re-run and verify the fix
suggestions.reason: The without_skill agent documented the bug analysis and fix well but omitted the re-run command. This is a meaningful gap: showing the command to verify the fix works is part of good debugging practice and the skill workflow.

The without_skill agent identified two root causes (null eval_output AND missing return in runnable) while the with_skill agent only addressed the null eval_output issue via run_and_evaluate. An assertion checking whether the agent identified both bugs (or just one) would help differentiate quality of diagnosis.

eval_feedback.overall: The without_skill run shows strong diagnostic ability — it identified both bugs and produced a correct fix. The only miss is not documenting the pixie-test command. 4 of 5 assertions pass.
